a.label: E = F_0(u)(w_1,...,w_k)
a.standard_term: multiquadratic extension field generated by the k radicals w_i = sqrt(u+N_i^2)

b.label: sign group H_sign = (C_2)^k
b.standard_term: elementary abelian 2-group of sign changes on the k radical generators (hyperoctahedral sign group)

evidence.label: (I1) Galois group of E over F_0(u)
evidence.summary: (I1) `[E : F_0(u)] = 2^k` with Galois group `H = (C_2)^k`, because the square classes of the `u + N_i^2` (distinct monic linear polynomials in `u`, hence squarefree and coprime) are independent.

evidence.label: generic normalization--reflection chain (thm:genericcover)
evidence.summary: Assume the square classes of $m^2+N_i^2$ are independent. Then: (i) $E/L$ is multiquadratic with group $H_{\mathrm{sign}}=(C_2)^k$; (ii) the stabilizer of $s$ in $H_{\mathrm{sign}}$ is trivial, hence $L(s)=E$; (iii) the
